item.title: Power struggle with CEO led to resignation of HDFC chair
item.description: Atanu Chakraborty quit the bank after a clash of strategy, personality and leadership styles with Sashidhar Jagdishan

item.title: Trump administration blocked from punishing Anthropic over Pentagon row
item.description: Judge says classification of group as ‘supply chain risk’ not aligned with stated US national security interests

item.title: Pernod Ricard and Jack Daniel’s owner in talks to combine operations
item.description: Discussions under way with Brown-Forman to create premium alcohol group as sector battles subdued demand

item.title: Tether hires KPMG as auditor ahead of US expansion
item.description: The world’s largest stablecoin issuer also brings on PwC to ready internal systems

item.title: The rout in UK and European bonds
item.description: What rising yields mean for everyday people

item.title: EU to impose fines on online platforms importing unsafe products
item.description: Repeat offenders could have their websites blocked in new customs rules aimed at curbing glut of cheap Chinese goods

item.title: Private credit’s moment for a healthy reset
item.description: Recent ructions highlight the case for better scrutiny of the asset class

item.title: US stocks drop, oil jumps by most in 2 weeks as Trump’s Iran deadline looms
item.description: US president repeats claim that Tehran ‘begging to make a deal’ but says he is unsure he will be able to find an agreement

item.title: Musk’s lawsuit against X advertisers thrown out by US judge
item.description: Billionaire had accused brands of organising an illegal boycott

item.title: Heathrow warns that third runway costs risk an HS2-style spiral
item.description: Airport proposes setting up oversight panel to keep project on track

item.title: Corebridge and Equitable tie-up goes for endurance more than flair
item.description: Unusually for a merger, the share prices of the two companies barely moved after the deal was announced

item.title: The US needs a low-cost airline consolidator, but JetBlue isn’t it
item.description: Heavily indebted, unprofitable carrier might not make an ideal partner

item.title: Mastercard looks to unwind biggest ever acquisition
item.description: Credit card group seeks to sell payments unit acquired for $3.2bn in 2019 for less than it paid

item.title: OpenAI makes a ‘Code Red’ turn in strategy
item.description: The company shows some business discipline in ditching Sora video app and plans for erotic chatbot

item.title: Jaguar Land Rover to shut UK plant for almost two weeks after supplier fire
item.description: Latest blow comes just months after £260mn cyber attack halted production

item.title: Odey fired executives for telling him to ‘sit in dungeons’ to protect women
item.description: Financier told court he objected to safeguarding measures, including sitting on a lower floor, firm imposed after misconduct claims

item.title: The relentless march of the omniscalers
item.description: Tech giants such as Alphabet, Amazon, Alibaba and Huawei are commanding enormous global resources

item.title: Apple to source parts made in US by Japanese supplier for first time
item.description: TDK to produce advanced sensors for smartphone cameras in reshoring push

item.title: UBS gates €400mn property fund for up to 3 years
item.description: Swiss bank said liquid assets in the Germany-based fund were insufficient to meet rise in redemption requests

item.title: Donald Trump’s policy swings are creating instability, energy chiefs say
item.description: Industry that benefited from president’s cuts to regulation and green policy is wary of unpredictability

item.title: European missile champion sees surging interest from Gulf as Iran strikes continue
item.description: MBDA plans 40% rise in production this year to meet soaring demand

item.title: NS&I boss forced out after payments withheld from bereaved families
item.description: Minister blames ‘tracing’ issues at state-owned savings bank for blunder affecting up to 37,500 savers

item.title: Digital euro project clears key hurdle ahead of European parliament vote
item.description: MEP in charge of assessing the project is due to circulate a report fully backing an electronic version of the single currency

item.title: Investors seek ‘pain point’ that pushes Trump into policy pivots on Iran war
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter, Scott Bessent discussed ways to recast ties between Fed and Treasury in BoE’s image

item.title: Can Europe break free from US tech dominance? You asked, we answered
item.description: FT Innovation Editor John Thornhill and EU correspondent Barbara Moens replied to reader questions in an Ask an Expert Q&A

item.title: Will software engineers survive agentic AI?
item.description: A data deep dive shows that job vacancies are rising — but only for senior developers

item.title: England’s housing at most affordable since 2015, official data shows
item.description: Bank of England deputy governor warns Iran war will take a toll, with quoted mortgage rates already rising

item.title: Barrick delays Pakistan mega mine as Iran conflict rattles region
item.description: Development of $9bn copper and gold project pushed back as Middle East war adds to worsening local security situation

item.title: Could New York’s climate rethink spread to other blue states?
item.description: Democratic officials are reassessing targets as concerns grow over energy affordability

item.title: Private credit rating agency hits back at ‘incendiary allegation’ by SEC
item.description: Egan-Jones claims US regulator harmed its business with publication of order this week
